# LAST KERNEL — Card Design Bible
**Version:** 1.0  
**Status:** Pending approval — do not edit assets until signed off

---

## Voice Standard

Every card description follows three rules:

1. **Under 12 words.** If it needs more, it's explaining, not saying.
2. **Dry, dark, no decoration.** Functional over poetic. The world is tired.
3. **Double duty.** One sentence does two things: tells you about the world AND hints at what the card costs or gives.

The world of Last Kernel is an underground district barely surviving a nightly siege. People are numbered, not named. Systems replace institutions. Warmth is a utility, not a comfort. Every card should feel like it exists in that world first, and on your board second.

---

## Status Key

- `KEEP` — Name and description are solid. No change.
- `POLISH` — Name works. Description needs voice.
- `RENAME` — Name needs to change. Description may also need work.
- `REWRITE` — Both need to change.

---

## 1. CHARACTERS

| Asset | Display Name | Status | Art Anchor | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Card_Baby | **Dependent** | POLISH | Bundled figure. No face. Curled tight. | *"The district counts it as a liability. The colony counts it differently."* |
| Card_Villager | **Drifter** | RENAME | Hooded, worn jacket, hands empty | *"No sector. No record. Still breathing."* |
| Card_Warrior | **Enforcer** | POLISH | Heavy coat, face plate, wide stance | *"The badge is gone. The habits aren't."* |
| Card_Ranger | **Scavenger** | POLISH | Lean, jury-rigged scope, short antenna | *"Past the perimeter twice. Both times she came back alone."* |
| Card_Mage | **Netrunner** | POLISH | Cable ports at temples, glowing eyes | *"She doesn't fight the signal. She becomes it."* |

**Notes:**
- `Drifter` replaces `Recruit`. A recruit implies a system that still recruits. This world doesn't.
- `Scavenger` replaces the current display name `Scavenger` — the name is already right, the description ("A sharp-eyed runner built for ranged work and salvage routes") is mechanical, not voiced.
- `Netrunner` is already named correctly. Description ("A field hacker who turns signal noise into weaponized breaches") is competent but forgettable. Replace it.

---

## 2. STRUCTURES

| Asset | Display Name | Status | Art Anchor | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Card_Bonfire | **Heat Coil** | POLISH | Coiled element on crude stand, orange glow | *"Everything that burns was something else first."* |
| Card_Hearth | **Burn Node** | RENAME | Wall-mounted heating unit, red indicator light | *"It keeps the cold back. That's all it needs to do."* |
| Card_Furnace | **Smelter Core** | POLISH | Industrial drum, molten glow from ports | *"Feed it ore. Wait. It doesn't care if you're watching."* |
| Card_Kiln | **Cure Oven** | RENAME | Sealed ceramic box, vent slots, heat shimmer | *"Clay in. Brick out. Patience is the only input it respects."* |
| Card_Anvil | **Fabricator Bench** | POLISH | Flat workbench, magnetic clamps, worn surface | *"The difference between salvage and equipment is about forty minutes."* |
| Card_House | **Hab Pod** | POLISH | Coffin-width pod, glowing door slit, stacked | *"Holds two. Smells like four. Keeps the cold out, mostly."* |
| Card_Grave | **Memory Casket** | POLISH | Sealed metal box, drive port on side, dim light | *"Not a memorial. A receipt."* |
| Card_Research_Library | **Kernel Archive** | POLISH | Wall of stacked drives, one screen still lit | *"Most of it is corrupted. The rest is worse."* |
| Card_GrandPortal | **Transit Gate** | RENAME | Heavy blast door, lock panel, amber signal light | *"It opens once per cycle. Make sure you're ready."* |
| Card_ClayQuarry | **Clay Extraction Rig** | RENAME | Drill arm over clay slurry tank | *"Slow, wet, essential. The district was built on this."* |
| Card_IronMine | **Ore Tap** | RENAME | Bore shaft, ore conveyor, single lamp | *"Everything down here eventually becomes something up here."* |

**Notes:**
- `Hearth` and `Bonfire` are functionally distinct but both feel like campfires. Renaming one to `Burn Node` creates a clear tier: improvised heat (Heat Coil) vs. structured heat (Burn Node).
- `Memory Casket` is already named. Description ("A sealed casket for bodies and backup drives. Closure is a system need.") — the last sentence is weak. Cut it. "Not a memorial. A receipt." says the same thing colder.
- `Transit Gate` replaces `Grand Portal`. Portals belong to a different genre.

---

## 3. MATERIALS

| Asset | Display Name | Status | Art Anchor | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Card_Wood | **Salvage Timber** | RENAME | Rough-cut beams, rope-bound bundle | *"Structural. Not elegant. Available."* |
| Card_Plank | **Deck Board** | RENAME | Smoothed planks, edge-marked | *"Two cuts from timber. One step from something useful."* |
| Card_Timber | **Load Bearer** | RENAME | Heavy structural beam, bolt holes visible | *"The colony stands because something holds it up."* |
| Card_WoodenStick | **Scrap Rod** | RENAME | Stripped rod, rough grain | *"The most honest object in the district."* |
| Card_Stone | **Slag Block** | RENAME | Dense grey block, compressed rubble texture | *"Heavy enough to stay where you put it."* |
| Card_Brick | **Fired Block** | RENAME | Uniform kiln-red brick, smooth faces | *"Slag, heat, time. In that order."* |
| Card_Clay | **Thermal Clay** | KEEP | Wet grey mass, thumbprint in surface | *"Soft now. Permanent later."* |
| Card_Fiber | **Wirecloth** | RENAME | Twisted synthetic fibers, spool-wound | *"Weak alone. The problem is it's never alone."* |
| Card_Rope | **Tension Cord** | RENAME | Braided cable, stress marks at ends | *"It either holds or it doesn't. You find out at the worst time."* |
| Card_Flint | **Striker Chip** | RENAME | Sharp-edged shard, one clean fracture | *"One chip. One spark. That's all fire asks."* |
| Card_IronOre | **Ferrite Vein** | RENAME | Raw ore chunk, metallic sheen, weight implied | *"Pull it from the ground. Prove it's worth something."* |
| Card_IronIngot | **Ferrite Bar** | KEEP | Smelted bar, still radiating heat | *"Hot, dense, and worth more than most people down here."* |
| Card_GlowingDust | **Signal Dust** | RENAME | Fine particles, faint cyan glow suspended | *"Nobody knows what it is. Everyone knows what it does."* |
| Card_AbyssalCore | **Void Cell** | RENAME | Dark crystalline core, radiating cracks | *"It shouldn't exist. It does. Use it carefully."* |
| Card_Corpse | **Shutdown Unit** | RENAME | Slumped silhouette, systems offline | *"Someone's colony lost a drifter. Your board gained one."* |

**Notes:**
- Wood/Plank/Timber form a clear tier. Names should make the hierarchy readable at a glance: Salvage Timber → Deck Board → Load Bearer.
- Clay stays `Thermal Clay` — it's already grounded and right. Description needs the polish.
- `Shutdown Unit` for Corpse: cold, specific, not gratuitous. It respects the world without softening it.

---

## 4. AREAS

| Asset | Display Name | Status | Art Anchor | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Card_Fields | **Blackout Yard** | KEEP | Dead billboards, open flat ground | *"Good salvage. Poor cover. Everyone's been here."* |
| Card_Forest | **Relay Graveyard** | POLISH | Fallen masts, live wire, dark overgrowth | *"Signals still move through it. In bad shapes."* |
| Card_Graveyard | **Memory Pit** | POLISH | Disposal trench, unmarked mounds | *"Some entries keep answering."* |
| Card_Highlands | **Dead Transit Line** | KEEP | Elevated rail corridor, exposed walkway | *"Fast routes. Exposed fights. Worth it if you move first."* |
| Card_Ruins | **Collapse Site** | POLISH | Toppled megastructure, buried systems | *"Valuable systems buried under hostile code."* |

**Notes:**
- Area descriptions are the strongest in the current set. Most need light polish, not rewrites.
- `Relay Graveyard` (the Forest card) — confusing because there's also a `Memory Pit` (the Graveyard area card). The art and loot table should clearly distinguish them: Relay Graveyard has wire/signal loot, Memory Pit has bodies/drives.
- Current description of Memory Pit ("A disposal trench for bodies, drives, and erased records. Some entries keep answering.") — trim the front clause. The last sentence is the whole card.

---

## 5. ENEMIES

| Asset | Display Name | Status | Art Anchor | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Card_Squirrel | **Cache Runner** | RENAME | Small, augmented, twitchy movement | *"It's been in your stores. It's been in everyone's stores."* |
| Card_Chicken | **Protein Drone** | KEEP | Round bird, subtle firmware ports | *"More firmware than instinct. Lays protein pods if kept alive."* |
| Card_Cow | **Nutrient Synth** | KEEP | Bulky, slow, passive expression | *"Useful until it wanders. Don't let it wander."* |
| Card_Crab | **Shell Crawler** | RENAME | Armored crustacean, industrial-weight claws | *"Hard to crack. Worth the effort."* |
| Card_Goblin | **Scrapper** | RENAME | Small, wiry, oversized salvaged knife | *"Hungry. Fast. Not wrong about how the world works now."* |
| Card_Slime | **Null Anomaly** | KEEP | Formless blob, corrupted pixel-art edge | *"It doesn't think. It just dissolves."* |
| Card_Satyr | **Reclaimer** | POLISH | Horned, salvage harness, built for speed | *"It strips living districts for parts. Yours looks lived-in."* |
| Card_TrollShaman | **Process Echo** | POLISH | Glitching figure repeating gestures | *"A corrupted pattern still running rituals it no longer understands."* |
| Card_CrimsonAcolyte | **Blackout Cultist** | POLISH | Cloaked, wired, crimson circuit marks | *"Wired into broken doctrine. It wants the Kernel silent."* |
| Card_DemonLord | **Kernel Sovereign** | POLISH | Massive armored shell, tendrils of dead wire | *"It wasn't born. It was left running."* |

**Notes:**
- `Scrapper` replaces `Glitched Raider`. "Glitched" signals a visual bug, not a threat. Scrappers are just humans who chose violence over colony life.
- `Cache Runner` replaces `Scrap Hound` for the Squirrel card. The squirrel mechanic is passive (not aggressive, drops food) — the name should reflect opportunistic behavior, not aggression.
- `Kernel Sovereign` description: the current "A rogue command intelligence wearing a combat shell. The district calls it the end process." is too long. It explains instead of landing. "It wasn't born. It was left running." says everything and leaves room for the player to be afraid.
- `Null Anomaly` for Slime is already excellent. Description is already right. Keep both.

---

## 6. EQUIPMENT

| Asset | Display Name | Status | Art Anchor | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Card_WoodenClub | **Scrap Baton** | RENAME | Reinforced club, wrapped grip, dent marks | *"The first weapon. Still works."* |
| Card_Sword | **Alloy Cutter** | KEEP | Heavy blade, worn edge, no guard | *"Made for close work. Makes close work faster."* |
| Card_Slingshot | **Pocket Launcher** | RENAME | Compact sling, hopper-fed, pocket-sized | *"Underestimated. Good."* |
| Card_Bow | **Tension Rig** | RENAME | Composite bow, salvage string, worn grip | *"Distance is a strategy. So is not missing."* |
| Card_Quiver | **Bolt Cache** | RENAME | Tube-carried bolts, quick-release mount | *"More shots. Simple math."* |
| Card_Staff | **Conduit Rod** | RENAME | Conductive rod, signal emitter at tip | *"It amplifies what's already there. Not always helpfully."* |
| Card_Cane | **Signal Cane** | RENAME | Elegant rod, embedded transmitter, handle grip | *"Subtle. Surgical. Expensive."* |
| Card_Tunic | **Wirecloth Wrap** | RENAME | Layered synthetic fiber garment, patched | *"Not armor. Better than nothing by exactly that much."* |
| Card_LeatherArmor | **Reclaimed Vest** | RENAME | Patchwork plates over worn leather base | *"Assembled from what the others left behind."* |
| Card_Chainmail | **Link Coat** | RENAME | Interlocked metal rings, heavy drape | *"Heavier than it looks. Worth it."* |
| Card_SlimeHat | **Null Cap** | RENAME | Matte headgear, signal-absorbing weave | *"It doesn't stop hits. It stops being found."* |
| Card_VitalityAmulet | **Vital Cell** | RENAME | Embedded biomonitor, chest-mounted disc | *"It tracks your heartbeat so you don't have to think about it."* |

---

## 7. RESOURCES (Produce-type)

| Asset | Display Name | Status | Art Anchor | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Card_Tree | **Salvage Grove** | RENAME | Dense trunks, rooted in cracked concrete | *"Rooted in old concrete. Still growing. Useful."* |
| Card_AppleTree | **Orchard Node** | RENAME | Gnarled tree, data-tag stapled to trunk | *"Tagged by the old sector authority. Still bearing."* |
| Card_BerryBush | **Cluster Vine** | RENAME | Dense low shrub, heavy with berries | *"Nobody planted it. Nobody needs to."* |
| Card_Rock | **Stone Deposit** | RENAME | Rock outcrop in urban rubble | *"Slower than ore. Free. That's the tradeoff."* |
| Card_Grass | **Ground Cover** | RENAME | Scrubby grass between cracked tiles | *"Not useful. Not nothing."* |
| Card_Soil | **Growth Medium** | RENAME | Dark enriched soil in a shallow container | *"The only input that doesn't ask what you're building."* |
| Card_IronDeposit | **Ore Seam** | RENAME | Exposed iron vein in a rock face | *"The mine sees it first. The smelter sees it last."* |
| Card_ClayPit | **Clay Bed** | RENAME | Soft exposed clay, water pooling at edges | *"Everything below this was once a lake."* |
| Card_BasaltColumns | **Basalt Stack** | RENAME | Geometric black columns, cracked clean | *"Geometric. Immovable. Worth harvesting."* |
| Card_PalmTree | **Canopy Node** | RENAME | Tall palm, torn fronds, coastal-urban setting | *"It survived the collapse. Take what it offers."* |

---

## 8. CONSUMABLES

Real food stays real. The contrast between ordinary food and collapsed-world context is intentional — it is part of the game's dry humor. Do not over-theme consumables.

| Asset | Display Name | Status | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Card_CoreRation | **Core Ration** | KEEP | *"Standard issue. Non-negotiable."* |
| Card_AlgaeWafer | **Algae Wafer** | KEEP | *"Tastes like the water table. Keeps you running."* |
| Card_VatBroth | **Vat Broth** | KEEP | *"It came from a vat. That's all you need to know."* |
| Card_MycoChip | **Myco Chip** | KEEP | *"Fungal. Nutritious. Unsettling. In that order."* |
| Card_SignalJerky | **Signal Jerky** | KEEP | *"Preserved by radiation. Not ideal. Effective."* |
| Card_RawMeat | **Raw Meat** | POLISH | *"Cook it. This is not a suggestion."* |
| Card_Egg | **Egg** | KEEP | *"Protein Drone output. Fresh is relative."* |
| Card_Milk | **Milk** | KEEP | *"From the Nutrient Synth. Still warm."* |
| Card_Apple | **Apple** | KEEP | *"The Orchard Node doesn't ask for much back."* |
| Card_Berry | **Berry** | KEEP | *"Grew without permission. Edible without cooking."* |
| Card_Coconut | **Coconut** | KEEP | *"High water content. Take it."* |
| Card_Potato | **Potato** | KEEP | *"Grows in bad soil. Reliable."* |
| Card_Turnip | **Turnip** | KEEP | *"Nobody's favorite. Everyone's fallback."* |
| Card_BakedPotato | **Baked Potato** | KEEP | *"The minimum. The reliable minimum."* |
| Card_Omelette | **Omelette** | KEEP | *"Simple. The good kind."* |
| Card_Soup | **Soup** | KEEP | *"Warm. That's the specification."* |
| Card_Stew | **Stew** | KEEP | *"Stew means everything edible is already in there."* |
| Card_Steak | **Steak** | KEEP | *"It took work to earn this."* |
| Card_FruitSalad | **Fruit Salad** | KEEP | *"Optimism in a bowl."* |
| Card_Milkshake | **Milkshake** | KEEP | *"Someone on this board has priorities."* |
| Card_RoastedAcorn | **Roasted Acorn** | KEEP | *"Emergency calories. Better than the alternative."* |
| Card_Acorn | **Acorn** | KEEP | *"Pre-roasted: no. Pre-edible: no."* |

## 9. CURRENCIES

| Asset | Display Name | Status | Art Anchor | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Card_Coin | **Scrap Chip** | RENAME | Irregular stamped chip, worn markings | *"The district's preferred medium. Untraceable by design."* |
| Card_Coral | **Reef Token** | RENAME | Smooth disc, coastal-sector marking | *"Island-sector tender. Worth less than you think. More than nothing."* |

## Summary of Changes

| Category | KEEP | POLISH | RENAME | REWRITE |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Characters | 0 | 4 | 1 | 0 |
| Structures | 0 | 6 | 5 | 0 |
| Materials | 2 | 0 | 13 | 0 |
| Areas | 2 | 3 | 0 | 0 |
| Enemies | 3 | 4 | 3 | 0 |
| Equipment | 1 | 0 | 11 | 0 |
| Resources | 0 | 0 | 10 | 0 |
| Consumables | 21 | 1 | 0 | 0 |
| Currencies | 0 | 0 | 2 | 0 |
| **Total** | **29** | **18** | **45** | **0** |

No rewrites — only renames and description polishes. The skeleton is sound; the voice needs sharpening.

---

*Approve this doc to begin asset pass. Changes touch `displayName` and `description` fields only — no mechanical data will be altered.*
